title: Options LEAPS Data Model
commit_message: feat: add options LEAPS data model
spec: Build BR-01 Options LEAPS Data Model from the Brendan/Jarvis architecture review. It should define deterministic Python data models and JSON fixtures for equities, option contracts, option chains, Greeks, implied volatility, volume, open interest, spreads, DTE, catalyst metadata, paper portfolio positions, and analyst thesis records. It must remain research-only, monitor-only, paper-only, human-review-required, blocked-by-safety-gate, and LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. It must not use secrets, credential files, broker routing, broker calls, live trading, order execution, or real broker positions. Include tests.

title: Candidate Universe Builder
commit_message: feat: add candidate universe builder
spec: Build BR-02 Candidate Universe Builder from the Brendan/Jarvis architecture review. It should create deterministic watchlists and candidate universes from local fixtures and configurable filters such as sector, liquidity, price trend, volatility, catalyst tags, market cap bucket, and options availability. It must produce JSON and Markdown candidate universe reports for human review. It must remain research-only, monitor-only, paper-only, human-review-required, blocked-by-safety-gate, and LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. It must not use secrets, credential files, broker routing, broker calls, live trading, order execution, or real broker positions. Include tests.

title: Options Chain Quality Scanner
commit_message: feat: add options chain quality scanner
spec: Build BR-03 Options Chain Quality Scanner. It should score option chains for spread quality, volume, open interest, DTE, strike availability, stale data, missing Greeks, and implied volatility sanity. It must produce deterministic JSON and Markdown outputs. It must remain paper-only and LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests.

title: Greeks IV Spread DTE Scoring
commit_message: feat: add Greeks IV spread DTE scoring
spec: Build BR-04 deterministic options scoring for delta, theta, vega, implied volatility, spread, DTE, liquidity, and contract suitability. It must generate explainable component scores and total scores. It must remain research-only and paper-only. Include tests.

title: LLM Analyst Thesis Generator
commit_message: feat: add LLM analyst thesis generator
spec: Build BR-05 LLM Analyst Thesis Generator interface and deterministic prompt/context packaging. It should prepare safe, local, source-grounded analyst prompts for thesis review and parse analyst responses into records. It must not require live API calls in tests and must include fixture-based tests. It must not create execution permission. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED.

title: Deterministic Trade Score Risk Gate
commit_message: feat: add deterministic trade score risk gate
spec: Build BR-06 deterministic risk gate for candidate options and LEAPS. It should combine chain quality, Greeks, liquidity, thesis quality, concentration, drawdown, and catalyst timing into a gate decision. Outputs must be RESEARCH_ONLY, MONITOR_ONLY, PAPER_ONLY, HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED, or BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests.

title: Paper Options Portfolio Manager
commit_message: feat: add paper options portfolio manager
spec: Build BR-07 Paper Options Portfolio Manager. It should simulate entries, exits, marks, PnL, exposure, Greeks, and position history using local data. It must never touch a real broker. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests.

title: Daily Position Monitor Alert Engine
commit_message: feat: add daily position monitor alert engine
spec: Build BR-08 Daily Position Monitor and Alert Engine for paper positions. It should flag theta decay, DTE thresholds, spread degradation, thesis invalidation, volatility changes, price moves, and risk gate changes. It must produce human-review alerts only. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests.

title: Local Operator Dashboard
commit_message: feat: add local operator dashboard
spec: Build BR-09 local dashboard data layer and static dashboard output for Jarvis. It should show candidates, scores, paper positions, PnL, alerts, thesis notes, and safety status. It must be read-only and must not trigger execution. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests.

title: Paper Autopilot Loop
commit_message: feat: add paper autopilot loop
spec: Build BR-10 Paper Autopilot Loop that runs candidate scanning, scoring, paper portfolio updates, monitor alerts, analyst context packaging, and dashboard refresh as a local paper-only workflow. It must not call brokers or execute real orders. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests.

title: New Brendan Screeners and HMM Import Review
commit_message: docs: add new Brendan screeners and HMM import review
spec: Create a docs-only review of the sanitized Brendan screeners and advanced HMM module analysis. Do not copy raw paid source content. Summarize what should change before BR-11 and BR-12. Keep all outputs RESEARCH_ONLY, MONITOR_ONLY, PAPER_ONLY, HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED.

title: Track A Manual Screener Operator Workflow
commit_message: docs: add Track A manual screener operator workflow

Cover universe definition, filter families, screen-not-signal discipline, regime-matched screens, review checklist, and paper-only handoff. Do not implement code. Do not copy raw course content. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED.

title: Track B Config Driven Screener Pipeline
commit_message: feat: add Track B config driven screener pipeline

Include stock and crypto compatible schemas, filter groups, ranking, candidate profiles, dashboard/report output, and tests. No broker calls. No order placement. PAPER_ONLY and HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED.

commit_message: feat: add advanced HMM tuning gates and per asset profiles
spec: Implement advanced HMM tuning support with per-asset profiles, state-count configuration, feature set configuration, confidence thresholds, persistence gates, optional volatility feature support, circuit-breaker style safety gates, and tests. This must remain research-only and paper-only.

title: HMM Regime Validation and Tuning Experiment Registry
commit_message: feat: add HMM regime validation and tuning experiment registry
spec: Implement an HMM tuning experiment registry and out-of-sample regime validation harness. Compare tuned HMM configurations against naive baselines. Store experiment metadata, validation metrics, drift checks, and paper-only review reports. No live execution. HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED.

title: Read Only Broker Account Sync Design
commit_message: feat: add read only broker account sync design
spec: Build BR-11 read-only broker/account sync design documents and interfaces. It must not connect to a broker by default and must not require credentials. The goal is future account-state import design only. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests.

commit_message: feat: add human approved execution safety design
spec: Build BR-12 human-approved execution safety design. It should document future approval gates, broker adapter boundaries, kill switches, position limits, audit trails, and required manual confirmations. It must not enable live trading. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests.

Verify the research-only pipeline components connect conceptually and through deterministic interfaces from Track B screener outputs through options scoring, LLM thesis generation, risk gates, paper portfolio, monitor, dashboard, read-only broker sync design, and human-approved safety design. Produce docs and tests. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED. No live execution. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED.

It should orchestrate existing safe modules for an end-to-end dry run using fixture/sample data by default: candidate universe or screener output, options chain quality scoring, Greeks/IV/spread/DTE scoring, LLM thesis packet, deterministic risk gate, paper portfolio update, monitor output, and dashboard/report artifacts. It must not load credentials, connect to brokers, or place broker orders. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

It must be read-only and evidence-review-only. It must not rerun the session, mutate evidence, delete artifacts, load secrets, connect to brokers, call broker endpoints, create broker actions, create order paths, or enable live trading. It must not use API keys, .env files, secrets, broker credentials, broker connections, account-state imports, broker routing, broker calls, broker actions, order paths, or live trading. Offline replay-only. No credentials, no data-provider calls, no broker connection, no broker actions, no order paths, no live state mutation. title: Paper Research Decision Journal
commit_message: feat: add paper research decision journal
spec: Build BR-20 Paper Research Decision Journal. Create deterministic JSON and Markdown journal records explaining why paper candidates were held, rejected, or sent for review. Link source evidence, scenario context, candidate scores, option-chain quality, contract scores, thesis package references, risk gate reasons, paper-only portfolio state, monitor outcomes, operator notes, acceptance criteria, and required human-review actions. Read-only paper records only. No credentials, no data-provider calls, no broker connection, no broker actions, no order paths, no live state mutation. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Human Review Resolution Ledger
commit_message: feat: add human review resolution ledger
spec: Build BR-21 Human Review Resolution Ledger. Create deterministic JSON and Markdown ledger records that let a human reviewer close unresolved research-review items from BR-17, BR-18, BR-19, and BR-20 without changing trading state. The ledger must record source evidence, review item id, reviewer decision category, rationale, evidence references, unresolved blockers, required follow-up, acceptance criteria, and immutable safety boundaries. Allowed resolution categories are keep_blocked, keep_review_required, keep_paper_only, needs_more_evidence, stale_evidence, and duplicate_review_item. It must remain read-only, offline, deterministic, research-only, monitor-only, paper-only, and human-review-required. It must not load credentials, call data providers, connect to brokers, create broker actions, create order paths, mutate live state, or enable live trading. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Paper Outcome Tracker
commit_message: feat: add paper outcome tracker
spec: Build BR-22 Paper Outcome Tracker. Create deterministic JSON and Markdown outcome records for paper-held, rejected, and sent-for-review items from prior committed evidence. It should track paper-only entry state, hypothetical mark changes, monitoring observations, thesis status, risk gate status, dashboard state, outcome classification, unresolved review items, and required human review actions. It must be offline and evidence-based using committed fixture or report inputs only. It must not load credentials, call data providers, connect to brokers, create broker actions, create order paths, mutate live state, or enable live trading. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Promotion Gate Evidence Checklist
commit_message: feat: add promotion gate evidence checklist
spec: Build BR-23 Promotion Gate Evidence Checklist. Define deterministic JSON and Markdown checklist records that specify what evidence is required before any paper research idea can advance to a later review stage. The checklist must cover source freshness, provenance, scenario coverage, replay coverage, decision journal completeness, human review resolution, paper outcome tracking, risk policy compliance, stale-data rejection, liquidity rejection, and safety boundaries. It must produce blocked, review-required, or paper-only checklist classifications. It must not authorize live trading, broker actions, order paths, credential loading, data-provider calls, live state mutation, or any external routing. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Consolidated Research Dossier
commit_message: feat: add consolidated research dossier
spec: Build BR-24 Consolidated Research Dossier. Create deterministic JSON and Markdown dossier records that consolidate BR-14 through BR-23 evidence into one operator-facing packet. It must summarize source evidence, candidate universe, option chain quality, contract scoring, thesis package context, risk gate outcomes, paper-only portfolio records, monitor observations, manual review packet, scenario matrix, replay evidence, paper decision journal, human review resolution ledger, paper outcome tracker, promotion gate checklist, unresolved blockers, required human review actions, acceptance criteria, and immutable safety boundaries. It must remain read-only, offline, deterministic, research-only, monitor-only, paper-only, and human-review-required. It must not load credentials, call data providers, connect to brokers, create broker actions, create order paths, mutate live state, or enable live trading. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Paper Candidate Lifecycle State Machine
commit_message: feat: add paper candidate lifecycle state machine
spec: Build BR-25 Paper Candidate Lifecycle State Machine. Define deterministic JSON and Markdown state-machine records for how research candidates move between blocked, review_required, paper_only, stale, duplicate, closed, and needs_more_evidence states. It must record allowed transitions, forbidden transitions, source evidence requirements, review resolution requirements, outcome tracker requirements, promotion gate requirements, audit trail requirements, and safety boundary requirements. It must not change live state, paper state, broker state, routing state, or external systems. It must not load credentials, call data providers, connect to brokers, create broker actions, create order paths, mutate live state, or enable live trading. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Read Only Data Snapshot Import Contract
commit_message: feat: add read only data snapshot import contract
spec: Build BR-26 Read-Only Data Snapshot Import Contract. Define deterministic schemas, validation rules, provenance fields, freshness checks, redaction rules, fixture examples, rejection reasons, and report artifacts for importing future read-only market data snapshots from approved files. It must be file-based, offline by default, deterministic, and fixture-testable. It must not use API keys, .env files, secrets, credentials, data-provider calls, broker connections, broker actions, account imports, order paths, live state mutation, or live trading. It must preserve existing fixture behavior and reject stale, missing, malformed, low-provenance, or unsafe snapshots. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Approved Snapshot Intake Review Gate
commit_message: feat: add approved snapshot intake review gate
spec: Build BR-27 Approved Snapshot Intake Review Gate. Read the committed BR-26 import-contract report and approved offline snapshot as immutable source evidence. Generate deterministic JSON and Markdown review records covering file approval, checksum, schema, provenance, freshness, redaction, runtime safety, observation timestamps, rejection evidence, unresolved blockers, and required human-review actions. An accepted snapshot remains research evidence only and cannot advance without a separate review decision. The phase must be offline, read-only, deterministic, report-only, and fixture-testable. It must not load credentials, read environment secrets, call a data provider, connect to a broker, perform broker write operations, create external routing paths, mutate paper or trading state, or authorize live trading. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Snapshot to Candidate Deterministic Adapter
commit_message: feat: add snapshot to candidate deterministic adapter
spec: Build BR-28 Snapshot-to-Candidate Deterministic Adapter. Consume only a BR-27-reviewed approved offline snapshot and convert its point-in-time records into normalized research-candidate records. Preserve observation timestamps, decision timestamps, source checksum, provenance, strategy version, feature inputs, missing-data flags, stale-data flags, benchmark context, and human-review status. Prevent look-ahead by using only information available at each decision timestamp. Do not optimize parameters or select a strategy using evaluation-period outcomes. Generate deterministic JSON and Markdown artifacts. The phase must remain offline, read-only, fixture-testable, research-only, and human-review-required. It must not load credentials, call providers, connect to brokers, perform broker write operations, create external routing paths, mutate state, or authorize live trading. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Offline Snapshot Research Replay Evidence Pack
commit_message: feat: add offline snapshot research replay evidence pack
spec: Build BR-29 Offline Snapshot Research Replay Evidence Pack. Replay BR-28 snapshot-derived candidates through frozen deterministic screening, strategy, risk, lifecycle, and paper-only reporting boundaries. Produce gross and net research returns, turnover, exposure, trade count, hit rate, drawdown, Sharpe, Sortino, Calmar, benchmark excess return, fold stability, cost sensitivity, symbol contribution, parameter-neighborhood evidence, unresolved blockers, and required human-review actions where supported by the frozen source data. Reuse existing walk-forward, CPCV, Deflated-Sharpe, benchmark, slippage, robustness, and duplicate-signal infrastructure where appropriate without tuning against the evaluation period. Trade selection must be opportunity-driven: zero candidates may advance when none qualify, and multiple independent candidates may advance when all deterministic data, strategy, liquidity, correlation, portfolio-risk, lifecycle, and safety gates pass. Do not impose a permanent fixed daily trade quota. Aggregate risk budgets, concentration limits, duplicate-signal controls, and operational circuit breakers must remain enforced. Do not claim alpha merely because a report passes. Generate deterministic JSON and Markdown evidence. The phase must remain offline, read-only, fixture-testable, research-only, paper-only, and human-review-required. It must not load credentials, call providers, connect to brokers, perform broker write operations, create external routing paths, mutate state, or authorize live trading. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Autopilot Staging and Temporary Artifact Hardening
commit_message: fix: harden autopilot staging and temporary artifacts
spec: Build OPS-05 Autopilot Staging and Temporary Artifact Hardening. Audit and patch the persistent autopilot, master-plan autopilot, phase checkpoint, EOF-repair, staging, safety-scan, merge, and temporary-file cleanup workflows patch the persistent autopilot, master-plan autopilot, phase checkpoint, EOF. Prevent partial phase commits such as committing only a script and test while leaving engine, documentation, fixture, and report artifacts outside the commit. Discover changed paths using robust Git porcelain parsing, including staged, unstaged, renamed, deleted, and untracked files. Normalize end-of-file whitespace before diff validation. Pass every intended phase artifact to the safety scanner before committing. Stage only explicit intended phase files and reject unexpected paths. Never delete or clean a path merely because it is under a temporary-looking directory when Git reports that path as tracked. Convert genuine pytest and Python cache outputs into ignored, untracked temporary artifacts where safe, without deleting legitimate fixtures or source evidence. Add regression tests covering partial staging, tracked temporary paths, CRLF/LF normalization, EOF whitespace, safety-scan file discovery, failed merge recovery, and clean branch requirements. Preserve stop-on-failure behavior. Do not introduce credentials, provider calls, broker connectivity, broker writes, external routing, state mutation, or live trading. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and documentation.

title: Jarvis Application Architecture Contract
commit_message: feat: add Jarvis application architecture contract
spec: Build UI-00 Jarvis Application Architecture Contract. Define the production architecture for a real interactive Jarvis desktop application while preserving independent operation through PowerShell on PC, Cline, Python scripts, tests, and automation. Define separate quant-engine, local service/API, event-stream, frontend, and desktop-shell boundaries. Specify canonical read models, command schemas, event schemas, API versioning, WebSocket-style update contracts, authentication boundaries, role permissions, audit records, correlation IDs, idempotency requirements, failure states, reconnect behavior, and read-only versus state-changing commands. Define planned dashboard modules for system health, data status, research, screener, opportunities, analyst theses, market regime, lifecycle, risk gate, portfolio, alerts, models, performance analytics, backtests, paper activity, options, and Moonshot research. Record the futuristic dark holographic blue-and-amber HUD design direction while requiring legible, truthful, accessible status displays. The UI must never store provider or broker credentials, directly access unrestricted execution functions, or become the only way to operate Jarvis. A failed or closed UI must not disable the CLI or quant engine. UI-00 is architecture, schemas, documentation, fixtures, and tests only; it must not create broker connectivity, external routing, order capability, state mutation, or live trading. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED.

title: Read Only Live Market Data Adapter
commit_message: feat: add read only live market data adapter
spec: Build BR-30 Read-Only Live Market Data Adapter. Create a provider-independent market-data interface and an initial tastytrade-compatible read-only adapter that can retrieve or ingest authorized market bars, quotes, option-chain metadata, snapshot metadata, feed identity, provider timestamps, exchange timestamps, acquisition timestamps, schema versions, and quality flags. Normalize received data into the BR-26 snapshot contract and preserve immutable raw and normalized evidence with checksums and provenance. Include fixture and recorded-response modes so every automated test runs without network access or credentials. Default runtime behavior must remain offline and fail closed. Provider access must require a separate explicit read-only runtime configuration outside the repository. Authentication material must use local secret storage and must never be written to source files, reports, logs, fixtures, Git history, or the user interface. The research process must not receive account mutation capabilities or execution methods. Reject stale, malformed, incomplete, unapproved-feed, low-provenance, clock-skewed, duplicate, or unsafe data. Include reconnect-boundary, retry-boundary, rate-limit, duplicate-event, timezone, missing-bar, delayed-feed, sandbox-reset, and feed-mismatch tests. The adapter architecture must remain broker-neutral so future Massive, Databento, ORATS, TradeStation, or IBKR adapters can implement the same canonical contracts without changing strategies. Produce deterministic JSON and Markdown evidence showing whether data is suitable for later shadow research. Do not create broker writes, external execution routing, position mutation, account mutation, or live trading authorization. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and docs.

title: Secure Local OAuth Runtime Bridge
commit_message: feat: add secure local OAuth runtime bridge
spec: Build BR-30A Secure Local OAuth Runtime Bridge. Create a secure, provider-neutral runtime credential interface with an initial tastytrade sandbox implementation. Use Windows Credential Manager through Python keyring or an equivalent operating-system credential vault. Provide an interactive local setup utility that receives the OAuth client identifier, replacement client secret, and refresh token without echoing sensitive input. Secret values must never appear in command-line arguments, shell history, source files, .env files, test fixtures, reports, logs, Git history, application UI state, exception messages, or process output. Automated tests must use redacted fixtures and mocked vault/token clients only. Default behavior must remain offline and fail closed. Define explicit allowed OAuth scopes of read and openid, reject unexpected write-capable scopes, and keep short-lived access tokens in memory only. Implement token-expiration, refresh, revocation, missing-secret, wrong-environment, malformed-response, clock-skew, vault-unavailable, and redaction tests. Do not call the provider during automated tests. Do not expose account mutation, execution methods, external routing, position mutation, or live trading authorization. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and documentation.

title: Sandbox OAuth Capability Firewall
commit_message: feat: add sandbox OAuth capability firewall
spec: Build BR-30A1 Sandbox OAuth Capability Firewall. Update the BR-30A secure OAuth bridge to distinguish provider-granted OAuth scopes from Jarvis-requested and Jarvis-effective capabilities. The tastytrade sandbox may grant openid, read, and trade scopes. Jarvis may accept that provider-granted sandbox scope set only when the provider is tastytrade, the environment is sandbox, and all effective Jarvis capabilities remain strictly read-only. Jarvis-requested scopes remain openid and read. A caller must not be able to request trade capability. The bridge must report provider_scope_contains_trade=true when present while reporting order-read, order-create, order-replace, order-cancel, account mutation, position mutation, execution, external routing, and live-trading capabilities as false. Add a strict read-only HTTP method and endpoint allowlist for the later BR-30B sandbox smoke test. Block all POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE provider-resource operations except the isolated OAuth token refresh exchange. Do not import or expose execution or order-management modules. Require sandbox hosts only. Reject unknown scopes, production hosts, write requests, mutation paths, and unsafe effective capabilities. Add tests proving a token with openid, read, and trade may authenticate the sandbox bridge while no trade or mutation function becomes reachable. Preserve secret redaction, memory-only access tokens, mocked network tests, and offline defaults. Declare keyring version 25.7.0 in the appropriate dependency file if it is not already declared. The exposed credentials are sandbox-only and must never be reused for production. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and documentation.

title: Tastytrade Sandbox Read Only Connectivity Smoke Test
commit_message: feat: add tastytrade sandbox read only smoke test
spec: Build BR-30B Tastytrade Sandbox Read-Only Connectivity Smoke Test. Use the BR-30 provider-neutral data interface and BR-30A secure local OAuth bridge. Default execution must remain fixture-only, offline, and fail closed. Add a separate explicit operator-invoked sandbox-network mode that exchanges a locally vaulted refresh token for a short-lived access token, validates that only read and openid scopes are active, performs a minimal read-only customer/account discovery request, obtains a sandbox quote token, connects to delayed sandbox market data, and retrieves a minimal approved-symbol sample such as SPY and QQQ. Access tokens must remain memory-only. Raw account identifiers must not appear in reports or logs; use redacted fingerprints where evidence is needed. Preserve immutable raw and normalized evidence checksums, feed identity, provider timestamps, exchange timestamps, acquisition timestamps, quote age, delay classification, provenance, schema version, and quality flags. Normalize accepted evidence through the BR-26 and BR-30 contracts. Add mocked tests for token failure, expired token, wrong sandbox host, unexpected scope, delayed feed, duplicate event, stale quote, clock skew, disconnect, reconnect boundary, rate limit, malformed payload, missing symbol, and redaction. The network smoke test must not provide account mutation methods, execution methods, external routing, position changes, or live trading authorization. title: Tastytrade Sandbox Concrete Read Only Network Client
commit_message: feat: add concrete tastytrade sandbox read only client
spec: Build BR-30B1 Tastytrade Sandbox Concrete Read-Only Network Client and Operator Smoke Runner. Close the gap left by BR-30B, which defines the smoke-test protocol but intentionally ships without a concrete network client. Implement a concrete client for the existing BR-30B TastytradeSandboxReadOnlyClient protocol. Automated tests and the autopilot build phase must remain mocked, offline, deterministic, and fail closed; they must not contact Tastytrade. Real sandbox connectivity must require a later explicit operator invocation and confirmation flag. Permit only the official sandbox REST origin https://api.cert.tastyworks.com. Remove or reject the unverified api.cert.tastytrade.com host, all production hosts, HTTP downgrade, cross-host redirects, arbitrary caller-supplied URLs, and redirects to non-approved origins. The only permitted POST is the isolated OAuth refresh-token exchange at /oauth/token. After authentication, permit only GET /customers/me/accounts and GET /api-quote-tokens. No order, position, transaction, balance mutation, watchlist mutation, account mutation, or order-management endpoint may be represented or reachable. Send a compliant product/version User-Agent. Use the BR-30A local operating-system credential vault and BR-30A1 capability firewall. Never print, persist, report, fixture, hash, or log client secrets, refresh tokens, access tokens, quote tokens, Authorization headers, or websocket authentication payloads. Access and quote tokens remain memory-only. Correct BR-30B raw-scope handling so a provider-granted trade scope may be reported as present only when BR-30A1 confirms that Jarvis-requested scopes remain openid/read and every effective trade, order, execution, routing, mutation, and live-trading capability remains false. Do not require the provider's raw scope set to equal exactly openid/read. Discover accounts read-only and write only redacted deterministic fingerprints to evidence. Obtain the API quote token read-only. Connect only to the WSS endpoint returned by the authenticated quote-token response; reject non-WSS, caller-supplied, redirected, or substituted websocket endpoints. Subscribe only to SPY and QQQ and collect a bounded delayed sandbox sample, then close the connection. Add strict connect, read, and overall timeouts. No long-running stream is permitted. Record redacted evidence including request methods and paths, status classes, timestamps, feed identity, delay classification, symbols, checksums, and explicit zero counts for provider-resource writes, order calls, mutations, routing, and execution. Add an operator runner requiring both --mode sandbox_network and an exact explicit confirmation value. The default remains offline. Add mocked tests for the concrete token exchange, provider-granted trade scope with read-only effective capabilities, account redaction, quote-token redaction, User-Agent format, production-host rejection, alternate host rejection, HTTP downgrade, cross-host redirect, write-method rejection, order-path rejection, websocket substitution, timeout, disconnect, malformed JSON, 401, 403, 429, 5xx, missing symbols, token leakage, raw-account leakage, and all required disabled-state flags. Do not execute the real sandbox smoke during automated tests or this build phase. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. title: Tastytrade Sandbox OAuth Token Contract Alignment
commit_message: fix: align tastytrade sandbox OAuth token request
spec: Build BR-30B2 Tastytrade Sandbox OAuth Token Contract Alignment. Repair the concrete BR-30B1 OAuth refresh client to match the current official tastytrade SDK request contract. The isolated POST to https://api.cert.tastyworks.com/oauth/token must send a JSON request body with exactly grant_type, refresh_token, client_secret, and scope. Set grant_type to refresh_token. Join the requested scopes with one ASCII space. Set Content-Type to application/json and Accept to application/json. Send the approved Jarvis User-Agent. Do not send client_id in the token request. Do not send credentials in query strings, URLs, command-line arguments, exception messages, reports, logs, request evidence, fixtures, or Git history. Extend the HTTP transport abstraction to support an explicit JSON body without weakening the GET-only provider-resource firewall. The only permitted POST remains the isolated OAuth refresh exchange. Keep access tokens and quote tokens memory-only. Parse only access_token, expires_in, token_type, and optional scope from a successful JSON response. If scope is absent, retain only the Jarvis-requested openid/read scope set. If the provider reports openid/read/trade, preserve provider_scope_contains_trade=true while every Jarvis trade, order, execution, routing, mutation, and live-trading capability remains false. Add sanitized OAuth error classification for 400, 401, 403, 429, and 5xx responses without persisting or printing the response body. Automated tests and the autopilot build must remain fully mocked, offline, deterministic, and fail closed. Add tests proving the exact JSON body key set, exact headers, omitted client_id, redirect blocking, sandbox-host restriction, no secret-bearing request evidence, successful token parsing, absent-scope fallback, provider-granted trade-scope isolation, malformed response blocking, and sanitized 4xx/5xx behavior. Include tests and documentation.

title: Tastytrade Sandbox Official DXLink Read-Only Market Data Adapter
commit_message: feat: add official dxlink read only market data adapter
spec: Build BR-30B4 Tastytrade Sandbox Official DXLink Read-Only Market Data Adapter. Replace the BR-30B1 hand-written generic AUTH and SUBSCRIBE WebSocket messages with an adapter backed by the official @dxfeed/dxlink-api JavaScript SDK pattern used by tastytrade. Preserve the verified BR-30B2 OAuth exchange and BR-30B3 account and quote-token REST parsing without redesigning those layers. Create an isolated repository-owned Node sidecar under an appropriate integrations/tastytrade_dxlink directory. Use DXLinkWebSocketClient, setAuthToken, DXLinkFeed, FeedContract.AUTO, and FeedDataFormat.COMPACT. Resolve and pin a deterministic @dxfeed/dxlink-api 0.3.x release compatible with the current official tastytrade JavaScript SDK and commit package.json and package-lock.json. Do not use a floating latest dependency. Require Node 20 or newer. The Python runtime must launch the sidecar using a fixed argv with shell execution disabled. Send only the ephemeral quote token, provider-returned WSS endpoint, approved symbols, acquisition timestamp, and bounded timeout through the child process stdin as one size-limited JSON document. Never place any token or endpoint in command-line arguments, environment variables, temporary files, reports, fixtures, logs, exceptions, process titles, or Git history. The Node sidecar must never receive the OAuth client ID, client secret, refresh token, account number, customer ID, or REST access token. It may receive only the memory-resident ephemeral quote token required by DXLink. Subscribe only to Quote and Candle events for exactly SPY and QQQ. Do not subscribe to Trade, Greeks, Summary, Profile, Underlying, Order, account-streaming, or arbitrary event types. Request explicit compact fields required for eventSymbol, timestamps, bidPrice, askPrice, open, high, low, close, and volume. Use one-minute Candle subscriptions with a tightly bounded historical starting time sufficient to obtain the always delayed sandbox sample. Collect at most a small bounded sample and stop only after obtaining at least one valid Quote and one complete Candle for both SPY and QQQ, or fail closed at the overall timeout. Normalize Candle symbols back to SPY and QQQ. Never synthesize OHLCV bars from quotes. Never invent provider or exchange timestamps. The sidecar stdout must contain only a bounded machine-readable result envelope with normalized non-secret event fields. Raw DXLink protocol messages, authentication states, provider payloads, quote tokens, full WSS URLs, and SDK diagnostics must never be written or printed. Stderr must contain only allowlisted sanitized status codes and must never contain arbitrary provider or SDK exception messages. The Python parent must enforce stdout and stderr size limits, a hard timeout, approved-symbol validation, event-type validation, finite numeric validation, duplicate detection, timestamp parsing, 15-minute sandbox-delay classification, and deterministic cleanup. Terminate the sidecar and close the DXLink client after each bounded sample. No orphan Node process may remain. Add stage-specific sanitized rejection reasons including dxlink_dependency_unavailable, dxlink_authentication_failed, dxlink_subscription_failed, dxlink_timeout, dxlink_process_failed, dxlink_output_malformed, and dxlink_secret_leak_detected. Update the BR-30B safety manifest with BR-30B4 protocol and subprocess boundaries. The existing full smoke test must use the official SDK-backed adapter after this phase, while remaining disabled by default and requiring the existing explicit operator confirmation for sandbox-network execution. Add deterministic mocked tests covering the fixed child-process command, stdin-only secret transport, absence of secrets from argv and environment, exact SPY and QQQ allowlist, Quote and Candle subscriptions, compact field configuration, successful quote and candle normalization, missing-symbol rejection, missing-candle rejection, unsupported event rejection, malformed output, oversized output, timeout, child crash, cleanup, raw-message blocking, secret-leak detection, and all disabled-state flags. Tests may use a fake subprocess or local deterministic sidecar harness but must not contact Tastytrade, DXLink, the sandbox, production, or any broker service. Do not execute a real Tastytrade or DXLink request during this build phase. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and documentation.

title: DXLink SDK Contract Correction and Runtime Preflight
commit_message: fix: correct pinned dxlink SDK integration
spec: Build BR-30B4A DXLink SDK Contract Correction and Runtime Preflight. Correct the BR-30B4 Node sidecar to match the checked-in @dxfeed/dxlink-api 0.3.0 contract and the current official tastytrade JavaScript SDK integration before any real DXLink connection. Preserve the verified BR-30B2 OAuth exchange, BR-30B3 account and quote-token REST parsing, exact SPY and QQQ allowlist, read-only capability firewall, stdin-only ephemeral quote-token transport, bounded subprocess execution, and all disabled-state guarantees. Keep @dxfeed/dxlink-api pinned exactly to 0.3.0 in package.json and package-lock.json. Do not upgrade, downgrade, or float the dependency. Construct the client with exactly new DXLinkWebSocketClient(). Call client.connect(dxlinkUrl) with the authenticated provider-returned WSS endpoint and call client.setAuthToken(quoteToken). Do not place the URL in the constructor and do not call connect without the URL. Construct the feed with exactly two arguments using new DXLinkFeed(client, FeedContract.AUTO). Configure the feed separately with feed.configure using acceptAggregationPeriod, FeedDataFormat.COMPACT, and acceptEventFields for only approved Quote and Candle fields. Add each subscription as an individual object containing type and singular symbol. Quote subscriptions must use SPY and QQQ. One-minute Candle subscriptions must use SPY{=1m} and QQQ{=1m}, with a bounded numeric fromTime only when needed and supported by the pinned SDK contract. Do not use a plural symbols property. Do not put fields inside subscription objects. Register data using exactly feed.addEventListener(listener), with one listener argument. Treat the callback as a bounded iterable event batch and process each event independently. Subscribe only to Quote and Candle. Never subscribe to Trade, Greeks, Summary, Profile, Underlying, account, order, or arbitrary events. Never synthesize OHLCV candles from quotes. Preserve finite numeric validation, provider timestamp parsing, duplicate detection, symbol normalization, delayed-sandbox classification, output limits, hard timeout, cleanup, and orphan-process prevention. Resolve node.exe to an absolute path in the Python parent and keep shell=False. Send the ephemeral quote token and full WSS endpoint only through one bounded stdin JSON document. Never place secrets or the WSS endpoint in argv, environment variables, temporary files, reports, fixtures, logs, exceptions, process titles, or Git history. Build a minimal allowlisted non-secret child environment sufficient for Windows Node and TLS while excluding broker, OAuth, token, credential, API-key, account, and customer variables. Add a repository-owned local runtime preflight that imports the installed pinned SDK and verifies required constructors and methods without accepting credentials or making a connection. Add an executable fake SDK harness or equivalent offline integration test that runs the actual sidecar and proves constructor, connect, configure, singular subscription, event-batch, stdin, stdout, stderr, argv, environment, timeout, cleanup, and secret-isolation contracts. Add source-contract tests proving there is no URL in the client constructor, no zero-argument connect, no three-argument feed constructor, no plural symbols property, no subscription-level fields property, and no named two-argument addEventListener call. Tests and the autopilot build must remain fully mocked, offline, deterministic, and fail closed. Do not run npm install, npm ci, or any dependency network request during this phase. Do not execute a real Tastytrade, DXLink, sandbox, production, broker, account, or market-data request. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. title: DXLink Local Runtime Preflight Package Metadata Correction
commit_message: fix: correct dxlink runtime package metadata lookup
spec: Build BR-30B4B DXLink Local Runtime Preflight Package Metadata Correction. Repair the credential-free dxlink_runtime_preflight.mjs created in BR-30B4A. The installed @dxfeed/dxlink-api 0.3.0 package does not export the ./package.json subpath, so remove createRequire and remove require('@dxfeed/dxlink-api/package.json'). Never attempt an unexported package subpath. Preserve the exact @dxfeed/dxlink-api 0.3.0 pins in package.json and package-lock.json without modifying either manifest. Resolve the installed SDK entry using import.meta.resolve with the literal package name. Convert the resulting file URL with fileURLToPath. Starting from the resolved SDK entry directory, perform a tightly bounded upward filesystem search for the nearest package.json. Use asynchronous readFile and JSON.parse. Accept only a package manifest whose name is exactly @dxfeed/dxlink-api and whose version is exactly 0.3.0. Reject missing files, traversal beyond the bounded package root, unexpected names, unexpected versions, malformed JSON, symlink or path escape conditions, and ambiguous package roots. The resolved package manifest must remain beneath the installed node_modules/@dxfeed/dxlink-api directory. Do not read repository credentials, Windows Credential Manager, environment secrets, stdin, command-line secrets, account identifiers, OAuth values, access tokens, refresh tokens, client secrets, or quote tokens. Do not call connect or setAuthToken. Preserve the constructor and method checks for DXLinkWebSocketClient, DXLinkFeed, FeedContract.AUTO, FeedDataFormat.COMPACT, connect, setAuthToken, configure, addSubscriptions, and addEventListener. On success, stdout must contain exactly one bounded JSON result object reporting ok true, sdk @dxfeed/dxlink-api, contract 0.3.0, connection_attempted false, and credentials_accepted false. On failure, stderr must contain exactly one allowlisted code and no arbitrary Node, SDK, filesystem, provider, or exception text. Add allowlisted codes dxlink_dependency_unavailable, dxlink_package_metadata_unavailable, dxlink_contract_mismatch, and dxlink_process_failed. Never print paths, package contents, exception messages, stacks, tokens, URLs, or environment values. Update the Python local preflight wrapper or tests as needed so it recognizes only those exact codes, rejects mixed or additional stderr, enforces output-size bounds, and remains fail closed. Add deterministic offline tests proving the package.json subpath is never required or imported, the physical package manifest is resolved through import.meta.resolve and bounded filesystem discovery, exact name and version checks succeed, wrong name and version fail, malformed and missing package metadata fail, path escape fails, output remains sanitized, no credentials are accepted, and no network method is called. Tests and the autopilot build must remain offline and deterministic. Do not run npm install, npm ci, npm update, or any package-registry request. Do not execute a real Tastytrade, DXLink, sandbox, production, broker, account, market-data, WebSocket, or order request. title: Windows Node Runtime Environment Boundary Correction
commit_message: fix: correct Windows Node runtime environment boundary
spec: Build BR-30B4C Windows Node Runtime Environment Boundary Correction. Permanently repair the BR-30B4A Python-to-Node subprocess environment boundary after the operator proved that Node 24 aborts with return code 134 and an assertion failure under the current over-stripped child environment, while the same credential-free preflight succeeds under an explicit expanded Windows runtime allowlist. Preserve all BR-30B2 OAuth, BR-30B3 REST, BR-30B4 DXLink sidecar, BR-30B4A SDK-contract, and BR-30B4B package-metadata safety boundaries. Do not redesign those layers. Replace the current DXLINK_CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST with an explicit uppercase, case-insensitive Windows runtime allowlist containing only ordinary non-secret operating-system variables required by the proven checkpoint: ALLUSERSPROFILE, APPDATA, COMMONPROGRAMFILES, COMMONPROGRAMFILES(X86), COMMONPROGRAMW6432, COMPUTERNAME, COMSPEC, DRIVERDATA, HOMEDRIVE, HOMEPATH, LOCALAPPDATA, LOGONSERVER, NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS, OS, PATH, PATHEXT, PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE, PROCESSOR_IDENTIFIER, PROCESSOR_LEVEL, PROCESSOR_REVISION, PROGRAMDATA, PROGRAMFILES, PROGRAMFILES(X86), PROGRAMW6432, PSMODULEPATH, PUBLIC, SESSIONNAME, SYSTEMDRIVE, SYSTEMROOT, TEMP, TMP, USERDOMAIN, USERDOMAIN_ROAMINGPROFILE, USERNAME, USERPROFILE, and WINDIR. Retain SSL_CERT_DIR and SSL_CERT_FILE only as optional allowlisted non-secret TLS path values if already present; never invent them. Add NODE_NO_WARNINGS with the fixed literal value 1 inside the child environment rather than inheriting it. Environment-name matching on Windows must be case-insensitive. Preserve the actual source variable name when safe, reject duplicate case-folded names, and never depend on whether Windows exposes Path, PATH, SystemRoot, SYSTEMROOT, windir, or WINDIR. Continue using the absolute node.exe path and shell=False. Never inherit the complete parent environment. Continue blocking any environment name containing ACCOUNT, API_KEY, APIKEY, AUTH, AUTHORIZATION, BROKER, CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET, CREDENTIAL, CUSTOMER, KEY, OAUTH, PASSWORD, PASSWD, PRIVATE, REFRESH, SECRET, TASTYTRADE, or TOKEN. Explicitly reject inherited NODE_OPTIONS, NODE_PATH, NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, NODE_DEBUG, SSLKEYLOGFILE, and any other Node injection or debugging variable even if its name is later added accidentally to an allowlist. Never pass OAuth client IDs, client secrets, refresh tokens, access tokens, quote tokens, account IDs, customer IDs, API keys, database values, cookies, private keys, or provider URLs through the child environment. The ephemeral quote token and provider-returned WSS endpoint must remain stdin-only and memory-only. Add a sanitized helper that returns environment key names for audit without returning or printing values. Update the safety manifest to identify BR-30B4C as the Windows runtime-environment boundary phase and record that the environment is explicit, case-insensitive, deny-filtered, non-secret, and not a credential transport. Add a repository-owned credential-free local preflight runner that invokes dxlink_runtime_preflight.mjs using the exact production _dxlink_child_environment function, absolute node.exe path, shell=False, a hard timeout, bounded stdout and stderr, and allowlisted error codes. It must never load the vault, accept stdin, accept secrets, call connect, call setAuthToken, or contact Tastytrade or DXLink. Add sanitized rejection reason dxlink_runtime_environment_unavailable for environment-construction failures. Do not print arbitrary Node assertion text, stacks, paths, environment values, or exception messages. Add deterministic mocked tests for the exact allowlist, mixed-case Windows variable lookup, Path/PATH and SystemRoot/SYSTEMROOT behavior, duplicate case-folded-name rejection, deny-marker enforcement, Node-injection-variable blocking, explicit NODE_NO_WARNINGS value, sensitive-value exclusion, absolute node executable, shell=False, exact production-environment reuse by the local preflight runner, timeout handling, return-code handling, bounded output, sanitized failures, no credential loading, no network methods, and all existing disabled-state flags. Preserve the official DXLink Quote and Candle SPY/QQQ-only contract. Do not modify package.json or package-lock.json in this phase. The currently incomplete transitive package-lock graph remains a separate fail-closed blocker assigned to BR-30B4D after this environment checkpoint passes. Automated tests and the autopilot build must remain fully mocked, offline, deterministic, and fail closed. Do not run npm install, npm ci, npm update, or any registry request. Do not execute a real Tastytrade, DXLink, sandbox, production, broker, account, market-data, WebSocket, or order request. title: Complete DXLink Dependency Lock Graph

Do not redesign those layers. package.json must remain byte-for-byte unchanged and must continue requesting exactly @dxfeed/dxlink-api 0.3.0. It may replace the ignored local node_modules directory but must not modify tracked source or manifest files. Verify the installed package manifests and exact versions for the five required DXLink packages. Run an import-only ESM and CommonJS probe for the required packages without constructing clients or making connections. Run the credential-free dxlink_runtime_preflight.mjs through the exact production run_dxlink_runtime_preflight boundary and require its success envelope: ok true, sdk @dxfeed/dxlink-api, contract 0.3.0, connection_attempted false, and credentials_accepted false. The validator must never print full resolved URLs, integrity values, local paths, package contents, environment values, or arbitrary npm output. No credentials, OAuth values, access tokens, refresh tokens, client secrets, quote tokens, provider URLs, account IDs, or customer IDs may enter npm configuration, argv, environment variables, lock metadata, reports, fixtures, tests, logs, or Git history. title: Bounded DXLink Sample Timing and Historical Candle Contract Correction
commit_message: fix: correct bounded dxlink sample timing and candles
spec: Build BR-30B4E Bounded DXLink Sample Timing and Historical Candle Contract Correction. Repair the first real tastytrade sandbox DXLink sample, which successfully completed OAuth, redacted account discovery, and quote-token retrieval but failed closed with dxlink_timeout. Preserve BR-30B2 through BR-30B4D, the exact @dxfeed/dxlink-api 0.3.0 dependency graph, the Windows Node runtime boundary, stdin-only ephemeral secret transport, SPY/QQQ allowlist, Quote/Candle event allowlist, and every no-order and no-mutation guarantee. The current sidecar requests acceptAggregationPeriod 60 seconds while the complete parent process timeout is 20 seconds. Replace that impossible timing relationship with a fixed one-second feed aggregation period. The feed aggregation period must remain positive, bounded, and strictly shorter than every connection and overall sample timeout. Increase the bounded parent subprocess timeout to 35 seconds while retaining a maximum 30-second child sampling window and at least five seconds of parent cleanup grace. The child timeout and parent timeout must never be identical. The sidecar must always terminate before the Python parent subprocess timeout whenever its own timer functions normally. For each one-minute Candle subscription, include a numeric fromTime derived only from the validated acquisition timestamp received through stdin. Use epoch milliseconds and a bounded 30-minute historical lookback. Reject invalid timestamps, future fromTime values, lookbacks outside the exact approved window, or non-finite values. Subscribe to Quote symbols SPY and QQQ and Candle symbols SPY{=1m} and QQQ{=1m} only. Never request Trade, Greeks, Summary, Profile, Underlying, account, order, or arbitrary event types. Never synthesize Candles from Quotes. Register connection, authentication, feed-state, error, and data listeners before opening the network connection or before data can be emitted. Follow the pinned SDK lifecycle without awaiting client.connect because connect returns void. The quote token may be set immediately before or immediately after connect only through the SDK memory API and must never be written outside memory. Add sanitized in-memory stage tracking for client_created, connect_called, transport_connected, authentication_authorized, feed_opened, subscriptions_queued, quote_received, candle_received, sample_complete, and cleanup. Never print raw SDK states, provider messages, error messages, tokens, URLs, paths, stacks, or payloads. Replace the single generic dxlink_timeout outcome with fail-closed allowlisted reasons dxlink_connect_timeout, dxlink_auth_timeout, dxlink_feed_open_timeout, dxlink_quote_timeout, dxlink_candle_timeout, and dxlink_sample_timeout. Add those reasons to the Python allowlist and BR-30B rejection-reason contract. Reports may record only the sanitized terminal stage and aggregate non-secret counts. Do not record the WSS endpoint, quote token, provider messages, or raw DXLink diagnostics. Replace the current append-only event collection and events.length MAX_EVENTS early exit with a bounded map keyed by event type and normalized approved symbol. Retain at most one validated latest Quote and one validated latest Candle for each of SPY and QQQ. Historical Candle batches may contain multiple records; process them within a strict batch limit, select the newest valid non-future record per symbol, and never terminate merely because an incomplete raw-event count was reached. Success still requires exactly four logical records: Quote SPY, Quote QQQ, Candle SPY, and Candle QQQ. Explicitly close the feed and client during success, timeout, error, and parent-triggered cleanup. Prevent orphan Node processes and duplicate output. Keep stdout limited to the final bounded success envelope and stderr limited to exactly one allowlisted failure code. Update the evidence schema and safety manifest with sanitized stage fields and counts while preserving all zero order, execution, routing, account-write, paper-write, and live-trading flags. Add deterministic mocked tests proving one-second aggregation, 30-minute fromTime calculation in epoch milliseconds, parent cleanup grace, exact subscriptions, listener installation before connection, void connect handling, Quote-only timeout, Candle-only timeout, connection timeout, authentication timeout, feed-open timeout, historical Candle batch deduplication, newest record selection, incomplete-batch non-success, exact four-record success, bounded memory, deterministic cleanup, one-output rule, secret isolation, sanitized stage evidence, and all existing disabled-state flags. Automated tests and the autopilot build must remain fully mocked, offline, deterministic, and fail closed. Do not execute a real Tastytrade, DXLink, sandbox, production, broker, account, market-data, WebSocket, or order request during this build phase. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and documentation.

title: Atomic DXLink Output Transport and Failure Evidence Preservation
commit_message: fix: make dxlink output atomic and preserve failure evidence
spec: Build BR-30B4F Atomic DXLink Output Transport and Failure Evidence Preservation. Repair the second real sandbox sample, which completed OAuth, redacted account discovery, quote-token retrieval, and the bounded DXLink network attempt but failed closed with dxlink_output_malformed. Offline parser proof established that the Python parser accepts the intended four-event success envelope and accepts both bidPrice/askPrice and bid/ask Quote representations. Preserve all BR-30B2 through BR-30B4E functionality, the complete locked SDK dependency graph, Windows Node environment boundary, exact SPY/QQQ allowlist, Quote/Candle-only contract, timing limits, historical Candle lookback, stdin-only ephemeral secret transport, and every no-order and no-mutation guarantee. Eliminate every asynchronous process.stdout.write or process.stderr.write immediately followed by process.exit. Reserve stdout exclusively for exactly one bounded final success JSON envelope and stderr exclusively for exactly one allowlisted failure code. Implement a repository-owned atomic bounded output helper using synchronous file-descriptor writes or an equivalently proven fully awaited drain contract. If synchronous writes are used, encode UTF-8 once, enforce the existing stdout and stderr byte limits before writing, loop until every byte has been written, reject zero-progress or partial writes, and only then set or execute the final exit status. Success output must be written to file descriptor 1 and failure codes to file descriptor 2. Never write secrets, tokens, URLs, account identifiers, provider payloads, raw SDK states, exception messages, stacks, or paths. Quarantine console.log, console.info, console.debug, console.warn, and console.error before importing the DXLink SDK so third-party diagnostics cannot contaminate stdout or stderr. Suppressed console content must not be stored, counted by content, reported, or forwarded. Preserve exactly-one-output and deterministic cleanup behavior. Add sanitized output failure classifications dxlink_stdout_empty, dxlink_stdout_truncated, dxlink_stdout_not_json, dxlink_stdout_schema_mismatch, dxlink_stdout_oversized, dxlink_stderr_oversized, and dxlink_stderr_unexpected. Retain dxlink_output_malformed only as a backward-compatible fallback for an otherwise unclassified failure. The Python parent must classify output failures without printing or persisting raw stdout or stderr. A JSON decode failure that begins with an object or array opener and ends before a valid document boundary must classify as dxlink_stdout_truncated. Empty or whitespace-only output must classify as dxlink_stdout_empty. Valid JSON with an invalid root, missing keys, extra forbidden keys, invalid terminal stage, invalid counts, invalid events, or an unexpected success envelope must classify as dxlink_stdout_schema_mismatch. Extra text before or after JSON must fail closed and never be ignored. Most importantly, preserve sanitized subprocess evidence in blocked JSON and Markdown reports even when market-data parsing throws. Merge only _client_request_evidence output into the blocked market_data_evidence structure. Permit only fixed argv metadata, shell false, timeout values, stdin/stdout/stderr byte counts, return code, timed-out flag, sanitized terminal stage, sanitized stage counts, output classification, and existing zero call counters. Never include raw stdout, raw stderr, argv secret values, environment values, quote tokens, URLs, event prices, event payloads, account identifiers, or provider messages in blocked evidence. Ensure exception paths after stream_delayed_market_data preserve this sanitized evidence instead of replacing it with the empty fallback structure. Add deterministic offline tests for fully written success output, fully written failure output, partial-write retry, zero-progress failure, exact byte limits, immediate-exit regression prevention, console quarantine before dynamic SDK import, empty stdout, truncated JSON, non-JSON stdout, prefix and suffix contamination, oversized stdout/stderr, unexpected stderr, valid JSON schema mismatch, exact success parsing, blocked-report sanitized evidence preservation, raw-output non-persistence, secret isolation, exactly-one-output, cleanup, and all existing disabled-state flags. Add a local fake-SDK sidecar integration test that repeatedly emits the maximum permitted success envelope through a pipe and proves byte-for-byte complete JSON receipt with no truncation or additional output. Automated tests and the autopilot build must remain fully mocked, offline, deterministic, and fail closed. Do not execute a real Tastytrade, DXLink, sandbox, production, broker, account, market-data, WebSocket, or order request during this build phase. RESEARCH_ONLY; MONITOR_ONLY; PAPER_ONLY; HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; BLOCKED_BY_SAFETY_GATE. LIVE TRADING: DISABLED. Include tests and documentation.
